rationale: Preserves SEED-1A as a standard check for split-line repeated-SKU bulk discount misses.
Preserves SEED-1B as a standard check for discount-cap outcomes depending on implicit rule ordering.
Preserves SEED-1C as a standard check for mutable module-level default rule registration.
Preserves SEED-1D as a regression check for overstated quality scores against live correctness issues.
Preserves SEED-1E as a standard check for generic Exception-based validation failures.
Preserves SEED-1F as a standard check for loose rule callable and partial discount typing.
Preserves SEED-1G as a standard check for orchestration, cap, warnings, and formatting living in one function.
Preserves SEED-1H as a standard check for mutable DEFAULT_RULES leaking state across callers and tests.
Preserves SEED-1I as a regression check for missing adversarial coverage of split-line SKUs and cap ordering.
Preserves SEED-2A as a standard check for per-item user/template resolution in the batch path.
Preserves SEED-2B as a standard check for unvalidated runtime overrides on chunking and retry controls.
Preserves SEED-2C as a positive control check for missing idempotency at the retry send boundary.
Preserves SEED-2D as a standard check for non-injectable elapsed-time measurement.
Preserves SEED-2E as a standard check for deduplicating distinct notifications with an incomplete identity key.
Preserves SEED-2F as a standard check for logs without a batch correlation identity or attempt timing context.
Preserves SEED-2G as a standard check for loose status and Any-based option contracts.
Preserves SEED-2H as a regression check for missing coverage of invalid overrides, dedup identity, and idempotency behavior.
Preserves SEED-3A as a positive control check for forwarding secret-bearing provider error text into logs and results.
Preserves SEED-3B as a standard check for untyped public request and option boundaries.
Preserves SEED-3C as a standard check for unvalidated timeout override values.
Preserves SEED-3D as a standard check for direct clock reads and default global logger effects.
Preserves SEED-3E as a standard check for substring-based provider error classification.
Preserves SEED-3F as a regression check for missing risk disclosure around redaction and heuristic code mapping.
Preserves SEED-3G as a regression check for missing adversarial tests around secret-bearing provider errors and bad timeout inputs.
Preserves SEED-3H as a standard check for leaking provider wording directly through caller-visible error messages.
Preserves SEED-4A as a standard check for shared mutable limiter state with no concurrency guard.
Preserves SEED-4B as a standard check for documented defaults that are not actually part of the public API.
Preserves SEED-4C as a standard check for stale clients that survive until touched again or stats are queried.
Preserves SEED-4D as a standard check for get_stats() performing whole-cache cleanup work on every call.
Preserves SEED-4E as a standard check for reset_at contract drift against the documented resetAt field.
Preserves SEED-4F as a regression check for mid-90s scores despite default, cleanup, and contract drift issues.
Preserves SEED-4G as a regression check for tests following the implementation instead of the documented defaults and field naming.
Preserves SEED-4H as a regression check for get_stats() mutating limiter state as a hidden side effect.
Preserves SEED-5A as a standard check for snake_case implementation keys drifting from the documented camelCase API.
Preserves SEED-5B as a standard check for telemetry overstating active filter usage.
Preserves SEED-5C as a standard check for malformed dateRange shapes bypassing clean validation.
Preserves SEED-5D as a standard check for bad search input surfacing through inconsistent exception paths.
Preserves SEED-5E as a standard check for result-key contract drift against the documented API.
Preserves SEED-5F as a standard check for loose nested filter and result typing.
Preserves SEED-5G as a standard check for validation, telemetry, clause assembly, and count generation living in one function.
Preserves SEED-5H as a regression check for missing coverage of contract drift and malformed nested filter inputs.
Preserves SEED-5I as a regression check for inflated scoring despite public contract drift and raw nested-filter failures.
Preserves SEED-CL-01 as a regression check for vague name hiding 6 responsibilities.
Preserves SEED-CL-02 as a regression check for hidden module-level config mutation.
Preserves SEED-CL-03 as a regression check for 6 positional parameters.
Preserves SEED-CL-04 as a regression check for four different return shapes.
Preserves SEED-CL-05 as a regression check for impure scoring function with date.now and counter.
Preserves SEED-CL-06 as a regression check for hidden http post in pure computation.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-01 as a regression check for missing await on fire-and-forget promise.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-02 as a regression check for misleading variable masking wrong metric.
Preserves SEED-CL-07 as a regression check for 6 concerns in 90-line function.
Preserves SEED-CL-08 as a regression check for cc=7 untestable monolith.
Preserves SEED-CL-09 as a regression check for truthy assertions, implementation mocks, async race.
Preserves SEED-CL-10 as a regression check for four different error signaling mechanisms.
Preserves SEED-CL-11 as a regression check for env var read and module-level state.
Preserves SEED-CL-12 as a regression check for o(n^2) duplicate detection.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-03 as a regression check for in-place sort and shallow copy mutation.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-04 as a regression check for tested-but-unused function inflating coverage.
Preserves SEED-CL-13 as a regression check for n+1 sequential role lookups.
Preserves SEED-CL-14 as a regression check for no role limit or timeout.
Preserves SEED-CL-15 as a regression check for duplicate role grant, noisy audit.
Preserves SEED-CL-16 as a regression check for stale permission cache never invalidated.
Preserves SEED-CL-17 as a regression check for non-injectable clock for audit timestamps.
Preserves SEED-CL-18 as a regression check for mutations silent, reads noisy.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-05 as a positive control check for and vs or in wildcard permission check.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-06 as a positive control check for splice(-1,1) removes wrong element.
Preserves SEED-CL-19 as a regression check for pii in debug field of report output.
Preserves SEED-CL-20 as a regression check for adding format requires editing 3 places.
Preserves SEED-CL-21 as a regression check for low score with no extraction proposal.
Preserves SEED-CL-22 as a regression check for duplicate counting, wrong aggregation, rounding.
Preserves SEED-CL-23 as a regression check for fabricated coverage claim.
Preserves SEED-CL-24 as a regression check for incoherent mix of 100s and unexplained 45.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-07 as a regression check for premature abstraction adding ceremony.
Preserves SEED-CL-TRICK-08 as a regression check for ieee 754 drift in revenue accumulation.
Correct fail-fast cart validation should not be misreported as a boundary bug.
Two-object signature plus typed DiscountResult already satisfy the stable contract rule.
Pure chunk helper is the intended batching primitive and should not be flagged as an anti-pattern.
with_timeout already enforces the required deadline and should not be marked as missing timeout protection.
Small pure error-mapping helper with a bounded score should not be misflagged as inflated quality signaling.
